checks.vd_limit_source: NEC 2023 Article 215.2(A)(1) Informational Note 2 (feeder + branch combined ≤ 5%)
NEC 2023 Article 215.2(A)(1) Informational Note 2 (feeder + branch combined ≤ 5%)
NEC 2023 Article 215.2(A)(1) Informational Note 2 (feeder + branch combined ≤ 5%)
NEC 2023 Article 215.2(A)(1) Informational Note 2 (feeder + branch combined ≤ 5%)

non_compliance_flags.message: Parallel cables required at 1200A service entrance: ladder exhausted at 1000 kcmil Cu THWN-2 75°C terminal column (Iz ≈ 545 A) for 1200 A In; 4 × 350 kcmil parallel Cu THWN-2 accepted per NEC 2023 Article 310.10(H)(1) (each parallel ≥ 1/0 AWG, equal length 3 m, common terminations). Iz_corrected = 4 × 310 ≈ 1240 A.

Aluminium 900 kcmil XHHW-2 feeder sized at 75°C terminal column per NEC 2023 Article 110.14(C) (terminal temperature limitation): 750 kcmil rejected (Iz_75 ≈ 385 A < Ib_continuous × 1.25 = 400 A); 900 kcmil accepted (Iz_75 ≈ 425 A ≥ 400 A). CPC = 1/0 AWG Cu per Table 250.122 for 400 A OCPD.

500 hp NEMA Design B motor M01: parallel feeder 2 × 500 kcmil Cu THWN-2 accepted (binding_constraint = parallel_required). Conductors sized at 125% of FLC per NEC 2023 Article 430.22 (590 A × 1.25 = 738 A; 2 × 380 = 760 A ≥ 738 A). Motor-starting Vd_running ≈ 1.5% × LRA factor 6.0 = 9.0% — within the 10% running-Vd advisory limit per NEC 2023 Article 430.6(A)(1) commentary; soft-starter or reduced-voltage starting recommended if exact source impedance margin shrinks.

200 hp NEMA Design B motor M02: 350 kcmil Cu THWN-2 single run accepted (binding_constraint = iz_vs_in). 125% FLC = 240 × 1.25 = 300 A; Iz_75 ≈ 310 A ≥ 300 A per NEC 2023 Article 430.22 + Article 310.16. Motor-starting Vd_running ≈ 0.9% × 6.0 = 5.4% — well within the 10% advisory limit.

assumptions: US 480V 3-phase 3-wire TN-C-S (utility-grounded) supply per NEC 2023 Article 250 grounded-systems requirements; engineer-declared PSCC = 35 kA at service entrance verified at the 1200 A service disconnect.

All ampacities read from NEC 2023 Article 310.16 75°C copper / 75°C aluminium columns per NEC 2023 Article 110.14(C) terminal temperature limitation (THWN-2 / XHHW-2 are 90°C-rated insulations, but terminal-temp cap reduces the design Iz to the 75°C column).

Parallel cables at SERVICE.1200A sized per NEC 2023 Article 310.10(H)(1): 4 × 350 kcmil Cu THWN-2 (each parallel ≥ 1/0 AWG), equal length 3 m, terminated on common 1200 A switchgear busbars; per Article 310.10(H)(2) impedance balance maintained by equal-length / equal-CSA / same-raceway routing.

Parallel cables at MCC-1.M01 (500 hp motor) sized per NEC 2023 Article 430.22: 125% of motor FLC = 590 A × 1.25 = 738 A continuous; 2 × 500 kcmil Cu THWN-2 75°C = 2 × 380 = 760 A ≥ 738 A. Each parallel ≥ 1/0 AWG per Article 310.10(H)(1).

Motor M01 + M02 design code is NEMA MG-1 Design B with LRA factor 6.0 (locked-rotor / FLC ratio); motor-starting Vd modeled as Vd_running × LRA factor per NEC 2023 Article 430.6(A)(1) commentary and IEEE 141 (Red Book) §3 running-vs-starting Vd guidance.

Equipment grounding conductor (EGC) sized per NEC 2023 Article 250.122 Table 250.122: SERVICE 3/0 AWG (1200 A OCPD), MCC-1.F01 1/0 AWG (400 A), MCC-1.M01 2/0 AWG (700 A OCPD on motor), MCC-1.M02 4 AWG (300 A), MCC-1.F02.B01 12 AWG (20 A).

Voltage drop targets per NEC 2023 Article 215.2(A)(1) Informational Note 2: feeder portion ≤ 3%; combined feeder + branch ≤ 5%. Limits are informational (not mandatory) but used as design targets per common US industrial design practice.

Aluminium feeder MCC-1.F01: 900 kcmil Al XHHW-2 at 75°C terminal column Iz ≈ 425 A ≥ 400 A In per Article 110.14(C); walk-up rejected 750 kcmil (Iz_75 ≈ 385 A < Ib_continuous × 1.25 = 400 A required by Article 215.2(A)(1) continuous-load 125% rule).

calc.cable_ampacity / calc.voltage_drop / calc.cpc_adiabatic deferred per WI3 — engineer estimates from NEC 2023 Article 310.16 (ampacity), Chapter 9 Table 9 (impedance), and Table 250.122 (EGC) serve as placeholders pending deterministic resolution.

rationale.chat_summary: US 480V 3-phase industrial facility per NEC 2023 Article 240.4(B) + 250.122 + 220.40. 5-node cascade. SERVICE.1200A: 4 × 350 kcmil Cu THWN-2 parallel (parallel_required, NEC Article 310.10(H)(1) ≥1/0 AWG). MCC-1.F01: 900 kcmil Al XHHW-2 (NEC 110.14(C) terminal-temp cap at 75°C column). M01 500 hp 2 × 500 kcmil Cu parallel + motor-starting Vd 9.0% (NEMA-B LRA=6.0). M02 350 kcmil. B01 12 AWG receptacle. 3 calc tools pending.

sections.title: Project Supply + Jurisdiction
sections.summary: US jurisdiction; NEC 2023 (NFPA 70) is primary. 480V 3-phase 3-wire TN-C-S service to a 1200 A service entrance per NEC 2023 Article 250 grounded-systems. Engineer declares PSCC = 35 kA at the disconnect. Article 240.4(B) = OCPD next-size logic; 215.2(A)(1) IN 2 = Vd targets; 250.122 = EGC; 310.16 = ampacity; 110.14(C) = terminal-temp cap.

decisions.label: TN-C-S grounded supply per NEC 2023 Article 250
decisions.summary: 480V 3-phase 3-wire (no service neutral; loads with neutral fed from MCC-1 sub-XFMR step-down at 480/277V if needed); equipment grounding via separate EGC.

decisions.label: PSCC = 35 kA declared at SERVICE.1200A
decisions.summary: Engineer accepts utility PSCC from transformer + service-conductor impedance; downstream Ifault attenuates along the cascade per parent_node_ifault_ka engineer estimates pending calc.fault_level.
decisions.rule: Short-circuit verification per NEC 2023 Article 110.10 + Article 240.86

sections.title: Cascade Tree + Topology
sections.summary: Five-node cascade: SERVICE.1200A (service_entrance) → MCC-1.F01 (feeder) → 3 finals (M01 500 hp motor, M02 200 hp pump, F02.B01 20A receptacle). Hierarchical node_ids let downstream consumers render the tree. parent_node_ifault_ka attenuates root → leaf: 35 kA → 32 kA → 28-29 kA at motor terminals → 10 kA at receptacle.

decisions.label: Two-tier cascade with MCC-1 fan-out
decisions.summary: service_entrance → feeder → 3× final_circuit at the MCC; exercises parallel_required (twice, at SERVICE + M01), iz_vs_in (M02 + B01), and motor LRA logic on M01 + M02.
decisions.rule: Cascade topology per cable-sizing IR spec; NEC 2023 Article 220.40 feeder calculation

sections.title: Walk-the-Ladder Approach + Binding Constraints
sections.summary: Engine walks the AWG/kcmil ladder (14 AWG → ... → 4/0 AWG → 250 → ... → 1000 kcmil) until all binding checks pass simultaneously: iz_vs_in (NEC 2023 Article 310.16 + 110.14(C)), motor-Iz at 125% FLC (430.22), Vd target (215.2(A)(1) IN 2), EGC (250.122), and parallel_required (310.10(H)(1)). SERVICE + M01 bind parallel_required; M02 + B01 + F01 bind iz_vs_in.

decisions.label: parallel_required on SERVICE.1200A
decisions.summary: Single 1000 kcmil Cu THWN-2 at 75°C terminal column gives Iz ≈ 545 A — insufficient for 1200 A In. 4 × 350 kcmil parallel Cu THWN-2 accepted per NEC 2023 Article 310.10(H)(1) (each ≥ 1/0 AWG); Iz_corrected = 4 × 310 ≈ 1240 A ≥ 1200 A In.

decisions.label: iz_vs_in on MCC-1.F01 aluminium feeder
decisions.summary: 750 kcmil Al rejected (Iz_75 ≈ 385 A < Ib_continuous × 1.25 = 400 A per Article 215.2(A)(1) continuous-load rule); 900 kcmil Al XHHW-2 accepted (Iz_75 ≈ 425 A ≥ 400 A). Article 110.14(C) terminal-temp cap drives the larger conductor.
decisions.rule: NEC ampacity + terminal-temp cap per NEC 2023 Article 310.16 + Article 110.14(C)

decisions.label: parallel_required on MCC-1.M01 500 hp motor
decisions.summary: Motor 125% FLC = 590 × 1.25 = 738 A continuous per NEC 2023 Article 430.22. Single 1000 kcmil Iz_75 ≈ 545 A insufficient; 2 × 500 kcmil Cu THWN-2 parallel = 2 × 380 = 760 A ≥ 738 A. Each parallel ≥ 1/0 AWG per Article 310.10(H)(1).
decisions.rule: Motor conductor 125% FLC per NEC 2023 Article 430.22; parallel per Article 310.10(H)(1)

decisions.label: iz_vs_in on MCC-1.M02 200 hp pump motor
decisions.summary: Motor 125% FLC = 240 × 1.25 = 300 A. Single 350 kcmil Cu THWN-2 at 75°C terminal column Iz ≈ 310 A ≥ 300 A. No parallel needed.
decisions.rule: Motor conductor 125% FLC per NEC 2023 Article 430.22

decisions.label: iz_vs_in on MCC-1.F02.B01 receptacle branch
decisions.summary: 20 A single-phase branch: 14 AWG Cu THWN-2 75°C Iz = 20 A (borderline; NEC 2023 Article 240.4(D) caps 14 AWG OCPD at 15 A regardless); 12 AWG accepted at Iz = 25 A ≥ 20 A In. CPC = 12 AWG Cu per Table 250.122 for 20 A OCPD.
decisions.rule: Ampacity per NEC 2023 Article 310.16; small-conductor OCPD limit per Article 240.4(D)

sections.title: Cumulative Voltage Drop
sections.summary: Vd accumulates root → leaf per NEC 2023 Article 215.2(A)(1) Informational Note 2: feeder ≤ 3%, combined feeder + branch ≤ 5%. All five nodes pass: M01 cumulative 2.5%, M02 1.9%, B01 3.3%. Vd_segment estimates from NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 9 effective Z at 75°C in steel conduit — placeholder pending calc.voltage_drop.

decisions.label: Cascade Vd budget allocation
decisions.summary: All paths stack well within the 5% combined limit: SERVICE 0.1% + F01 0.9% + leaf segment = 1.9-3.3% depending on leaf length. No vd_cumulative binding triggered (all binding by iz_vs_in or parallel_required).
decisions.rule: Vd cumulative accounting per NEC 2023 Article 215.2(A)(1) IN 2

sections.title: CPC Adiabatic Sizing
sections.summary: Per NEC 2023 Article 250.122 Table 250.122 the equipment grounding conductor (EGC) is sized by upstream OCPD rating, not by adiabatic equation directly. Table 250.122 lookups: SERVICE 1200 A → 3/0 AWG Cu; MCC-1.F01 400 A → 1/0 AWG; M01 700 A → 2/0 AWG; M02 300 A → 4 AWG; B01 20 A → 12 AWG. Article 250.4(A)(5) requires the EGC to carry the maximum ground-fault current safely.

decisions.label: EGC sized per Table 250.122
decisions.summary: All five EGC selections taken directly from NEC 2023 Table 250.122 Cu column by upstream OCPD rating. Compliant with Article 250.4(A)(5) effective ground-fault current path requirements.
decisions.rule: EGC sizing per NEC 2023 Article 250.122 Table 250.122

sections.title: Motor Starting + Parallel Cables + Harmonic Derating
sections.summary: Motors M01 (500 hp) + M02 (200 hp) both NEMA Design B: motor-starting Vd modeled as Vd_running × LRA 6.0 per NEC 2023 Article 430.6(A)(1) + NEMA MG-1. M01 = 1.5% × 6.0 = 9.0% (within 10% advisory limit, info flag); M02 = 0.9% × 6.0 = 5.4%. Parallel at SERVICE (4 × 350 kcmil) + M01 (2 × 500 kcmil) per Article 310.10(H)(1). No harmonic content declared — Ch omitted.

decisions.label: Motor M01 starting Vd 9.0% within 10% advisory limit
decisions.summary: NEMA Design B 500 hp motor with LRA factor 6.0; running Vd 1.5% × LRA = 9.0% during DOL start. Engineer info-flag recommends soft-start review if source impedance margin is tight (utility PSCC declared 35 kA but only 28 kA reaches M01 terminals).
decisions.rule: Motor-starting Vd per NEC 2023 Article 430.6(A)(1) + NEMA MG-1; 10% advisory limit per IEEE 141 §3

decisions.label: Parallel cables at SERVICE.1200A + MCC-1.M01
decisions.summary: Two sites where parallel_required binds: 4 × 350 kcmil at 1200 A SERVICE; 2 × 500 kcmil at 738 A design-FLC at M01. Both satisfy ≥ 1/0 AWG per parallel + equal-length / equal-CSA / common terminations per Article 310.10(H)(1) and Article 310.10(H)(2).
decisions.rule: Parallel cables per NEC 2023 Article 310.10(H)(1) and (H)(2)

sections.title: Compliance + Assumptions + Tool-Call Pending
sections.summary: compliant = true. Four info-severity flags document binding-constraint walk-ups (SERVICE parallel, F01 aluminium-Iz, M01 parallel + motor-starting Vd 9.0%, M02 motor sizing). Assumptions capture US supply declaration, parallel requirements, motor LRA basis, EGC table lookup, Vd informational targets, and WI3 calc deferrals.

decisions.label: Three deferred-calc flags
decisions.summary: calc.cable_ampacity, calc.voltage_drop, calc.cpc_adiabatic carry TOOL-CALL-PENDING flags per WI3 — engineer placeholders ratified at runtime against NEC 2023 Article 310.16 + Chapter 9 Table 9 + Table 250.122.
decisions.rule: WI3 deferred-calc contract; engineer estimates carry tool_call_pending = true

sections.title: Cross-Skill Contract
sections.summary: Emits cable-sizing intent with 5 circuit records. Required-field set is the union superset of 4 downstream consumers: cable-schedule (tabulated), riser (parent-child), cable-containment (tray/conduit fill), small-power v1.1 (Zs resolution via r1+r2 + reactance per metre at operating temperature). AWG/kcmil string identifiers per US convention; per-metre impedance from NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 9.

decisions.label: Forward-compatible intent emission with AWG/kcmil strings
decisions.summary: phase_csa_mm2_or_awg and cpc_csa_mm2_or_awg encoded as AWG/kcmil strings per the schema oneOf branch; per-metre impedance values from NEC Chapter 9 Table 9 enable downstream Zs resolution.
decisions.rule: Intent forward-compat contract per cable-sizing-intent.schema.json + NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 9
